headline: pki.schema.c509.encode produces C509 CBOR certificates -- a DER X.509 certificate compresses to a compact, byte-exact-invertible type-3 C509, and a deterministic-CBOR encoder joins pki.cbor.
summary: The C509 producing side. pki.schema.c509.encode is the byte-exact inverse of pki.schema.c509.parse (draft-ietf-cose-cbor-encoded-cert). Given a DER X.509 v3 certificate it forward-transforms it to a compact type-3 C509 -- the CBOR re-encoding of the certificate whose signature is copied from the source and re-expressed as a fixed-width r||s -- and self-verifies the transform round-trips: parse(encode(der)).reconstructedDer reproduces the original DER byte for byte, so the original signature still verifies. Given a c509.parse result it re-emits the native CBOR array. The emission is canonical deterministic CBOR (RFC 8949 section 4.2) -- shortest-form heads, definite lengths, sorted map keys, and the registry integer shorthand for every registered algorithm, attribute, and extension -- and applies the C509 compressions (a MAC-address commonName to a tag-48 EUI, a lone keyUsage to its integer shortcut, an EC point to its sign-of-y marker). It is signing-free, and a certificate outside the invertible covered set (a v1 certificate, a non-ECDSA signature, an unsupported curve) fails closed with a typed C509Error rather than a lossy re-encoding. Underpinning it, pki.cbor gains a deterministic-CBOR encoder (pki.cbor.build) -- the byte-exact inverse of the strict decoder, so encoded output always re-decodes. Certificate parsing remains pki.schema.c509.parse.

Added: pki.schema.c509.encode(input) encodes a C509 certificate to deterministic-CBOR bytes -- a DER X.509 v3 certificate to a compact type-3 C509 (byte-exact-invertible, so the original signature verifies), or a pki.schema.c509.parse result re-emitted to its native array. Canonical deterministic CBOR with the registry integer shorthands and the C509 compressions; a certificate outside the invertible covered set throws a typed C509Error. Certificate parsing remains pki.schema.c509.parse.

pki.cbor.build is a deterministic-CBOR encoder (RFC 8949 section 4.2) -- the byte-exact inverse of pki.cbor.decode: shortest-form heads, definite lengths, sorted and unique map keys, over unsigned and negative integers, byte and text strings, arrays, maps, tags, and the tagged bignum / epoch-time / object-identifier leaves. Encoded output always re-decodes through the strict decoder.
